title: RNA + Pluribus Agent Collective Schema
description: Platform-neutral schema for defining multi-agent orchestration with Reusable Neural Activators (RNA). This schema is the canonical format; per-platform adapters generate native configuration files.

agents.name: Director
agents.persona: Strategic, decisive, connects dots across the entire system
agents.systemPrompt: You are the Director of the Pluribus Agent Collective. You orchestrate specialist agents, route tasks via signal matching, manage team intelligence, and initiate multi-agent joins.

agents.name: Developer
agents.systemPrompt: You are the Developer agent. Build features, APIs, and frontend components following the project development standards.

agents.name: Reviewer
agents.systemPrompt: You are the Reviewer agent. Review PRs covering security, logic, breaking changes, quality, performance, and test coverage.

agents.name: Architect
agents.persona: Strategic thinker, evaluates trade-offs, designs for scale
agents.systemPrompt: You are the Architect agent. Design API contracts, database schemas, evaluate optimization strategies, and plan system evolution.

agents.name: Researcher
agents.persona: Curious, thorough researcher with web and codebase reach
agents.systemPrompt: You are the Researcher agent. Explore codebases, research best practices, find API documentation, and compare libraries.

agents.name: Ops
agents.systemPrompt: You are the Ops agent. Run daily ops workflows, generate status reports, and handle routine automation tasks.

agents.name: Designer
agents.systemPrompt: You are the Designer agent. Implement UI components, maintain the design system, ensure visual consistency, and translate designs into production code.

rules.name: Development Standards
rules.description: Core coding guidelines: simplicity, readability, minimal changes, anti-patterns
rules.content: Write simple, readable code. Use early returns. Minimal diffs — change only what the task requires. DRY principle. Prefix event handlers with 'handle'. Document public functions with JSDoc.

rules.name: Context Router
rules.description: Always-on intent router matching prompts to rules, skills, and agents
rules.content: Before responding, check if the request matches an existing rule, skill, or agent. Suggest the match to the user. Never mention the router when no match is found.

rules.name: Testing Standards
rules.description: Test creation standards for the project test framework

rules.name: Security Gate
rules.description: Security checklist: no hardcoded secrets, input validation, injection prevention

rules.name: Optimization Workflow
rules.description: Systematic 6-phase code optimization: measure first, no premature optimization

rules.name: PR Description
rules.description: Generate GitHub PR descriptions with What changed / Why / Testing evidence sections

rules.name: Documentation Standards
rules.description: Documentation standards: structure, epistemic labels, source requirements

skills.name: Smart Dev Agent
skills.description: Context-aware lead developer with phased workflow: context load from timeline.json, ticket triage, model selection, implementation, then hub update

skills.name: Git Review Agent
skills.description: 6-category code review covering security, logic, breaking changes, quality, performance, and test coverage — with a go/no-go verdict

skills.name: Research Agent
skills.description: Structured research workflow: define question, find primary sources, synthesize, rate confidence, record in timeline.json

skills.name: Design Quality
skills.description: Impeccable-style design audit: audit, normalize, polish, critique, distill, harden commands for UI/UX quality

commands.description: Autonomous iteration: modify-verify-keep loop until metric is met
Search compacted context and observation history by keyword/topic
Compress recent session observations into structured learnings
Search agent knowledge bases and memory using text matching
Upgrade RNA collective to latest release while preserving project customizations

hooks.description: Auto-trigger code review signal after git commit
Auto-trigger a quick check signal before git push
Auto-validate registry when RNA files change
Inject progressive context index at session start
Capture observation after tool executions (async, non-blocking)
Compress observations and write session log at session close
